question.text: A short call position at strike 83 with premium received Rs. 0.10. At expiry USDINR is 83.40. What is the net P&L?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Short call: Premium received = 0.10. Payoff to buyer (seller's loss) = 83.40 - 83 = 0.40. Net = 0.10 - 0.40 = -0.30 (loss). **The Trap:** The seller keeps the premium (0.10) but must pay the intrinsic value (0.40). Net = loss of 0.30. **Cross-Exam Context:** Short option P&L is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Short call profit only when spot stays at or below strike. Beyond BEP (83.10), every paisa up is a loss.
